Module_4 assignment - Flask app db backend with tests documentation

Add your content using reStructuredText syntax. See the reStructuredText documentation for details.

app.create_app(test_config=None)

Create and configure the Flask application.

Parameters:

test_config (dict | None) – Optional dict of Flask config overrides for testing.

Returns:

Configured Flask app with analysis and data-pull routes.

Return type:

flask.Flask

app.finish_pull_data() → None

Release the pull-data lock after a scrape/load run completes.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

app.format_analysis_value(value)

Format query result values for HTML display.

Parameters:

value (object) – Scalar, tuple, or list from a query result.

Returns:

Formatted value with floats rounded to two decimal places.

Return type:

object

app.get_database_url() → str

Return the PostgreSQL connection URL for the Flask app.

Returns:

DATABASE_URL environment variable, or a local default.

Return type:

str

app.get_db_connection(dbname=None, user=None)

Open a PostgreSQL connection using URL or explicit credentials.

Parameters:

  • dbname (str | None) – Optional database name override.

  • user (str | None) – Optional database user override.

Returns:

Active psycopg connection.

Return type:

psycopg.Connection

app.get_latest_added_on(path: Path) → str | None

Find the most recent added_on date in a JSON record file.

Parameters:

path (pathlib.Path) – Path to the JSON record file.

Returns:

Latest date as YYYY-MM-DD, or None if none found.

Return type:

str | None

app.is_pull_data_running() → bool

Check whether a pull-data job is currently in progress.

Returns:

True if pull data is running.

Return type:

bool

app.load_watermark() → str | None

Load the saved minimum added_on date from the watermark file.

Returns:

ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD), or None if missing.

Return type:

str | None

app.parse_added_on_date(value: str)

Parse a Grad Cafe added_on date string.

Parameters:

value (str) – Date string in long or abbreviated month format.

Returns:

Parsed date, or None if empty or unparseable.

Return type:

datetime.date | None

app.read_json_records(path: Path)

Read applicant records from a JSON file.

Parameters:

path (pathlib.Path) – Path to a JSON file containing a list or {"rows": [...]}.

Returns:

List of record dictionaries; empty list if file is missing.

Return type:

list[dict]

app.run_python_script(script_path: Path, *args: str)

Run a Python script as a subprocess and capture its output.

Parameters:

  • script_path (pathlib.Path) – Path to the script to execute.

  • args (str) – Additional command-line arguments.

Returns:

Completed subprocess result.

Return type:

subprocess.CompletedProcess

app.save_watermark(value: str) → None

Persist the latest added_on date to the watermark file.

Parameters:

value (str) – ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD) to store.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

app.try_start_pull_data() → bool

Attempt to acquire the pull-data lock.

Returns:

True if the lock was acquired; False if a pull is already in progress.

Return type:

bool

query_data.convert_decimal(value)

Convert PostgreSQL Decimal values to float for display.

Parameters:

value (object) – Query result cell value.

Returns:

float if value is a Decimal; otherwise value.

Return type:

object

query_data.execute_query(connection, query)

Execute a SQL query and return a formatted result.

Parameters:

  • connection (psycopg.Connection) – Open psycopg database connection.

  • query (str) – SQL statement to execute.

Returns:

Scalar, tuple, or list depending on row/column count; None if no rows returned.

Return type:

object | None

query_data.get_all_query_results(connection)

Run all analysis queries and pair each with its question text.

Parameters:

connection (psycopg.Connection) – Open psycopg database connection (closed on return).

Returns:

List of (question, answer) tuples.

Return type:

list[tuple[str, object]]

query_data.get_connection()

Open a connection to the applicant database.

Uses DATABASE_URL when available; otherwise connects to the local applicant_db database as postgres.

Returns:

Active psycopg connection.

Return type:

psycopg.Connection

query_data.get_database_url()

Return the DATABASE_URL environment variable if set.

Returns:

PostgreSQL connection URL, or None if unset.

Return type:

str | None

create_database.start_postgres()

Start the local PostgreSQL server if it is not already running.

Skips startup when DATABASE_URL is set (remote database in use).

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

Raises:

RuntimeError – If pg_ctl start exits with a non-zero code.

load_data.clean_obj(obj)

Recursively normalize string encoding in nested JSON structures.

Parameters:

obj (object) – JSON-compatible value (dict, list, str, or scalar).

Returns:

Copy of obj with strings re-encoded via cp1252.

Return type:

object

load_data.get_db_config()

Return database connection settings from the environment or defaults.

Returns:

DATABASE_URL connection string if set, otherwise the DB_CONFIG dictionary.

Return type:

str | dict

load_data.load_json_to_postgres(json_file='C:\\Users\\hz98yb\\Training_Files\\jhu_software_concepts\\module_3\\json_files\\applicant_data_updated_cleaned.json', table_name='applicants', delete_date=None)

Load applicant records from JSON/JSONL into a PostgreSQL table.

Creates the destination table and unique index if they do not exist. Optionally deletes existing rows for a given date before inserting.

Parameters:

  • json_file (str | pathlib.Path) – Path to the JSON or JSONL input file.

  • table_name (str) – Destination PostgreSQL table name.

  • delete_date (str | None) – If set, delete rows with this date_added (YYYY-MM-DD) before insert.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

load_data.normalize_records(parsed)

Convert parsed JSON into a flat list of cleaned record dicts.

Parameters:

parsed (dict | list) – Top-level JSON object or list from a file.

Returns:

List of applicant record dictionaries.

Return type:

list[dict]

Raises:

TypeError – If parsed is neither a dict nor a list.

load_data.parse_date(value)

Parse a date string in "%B %d, %Y" format.

Parameters:

value (object) – Date string (e.g. "June 12, 2026").

Returns:

Parsed date, or None if empty or invalid.

Return type:

datetime.date | None

load_data.parse_float(value)

Parse a value as a float, returning None on failure.

Parameters:

value (object) – Raw field value from a JSON record.

Returns:

Parsed float, or None if empty or invalid.

Return type:

float | None

load_data.pick(row, *keys)

Return the first non-empty value for any of the given keys.

Parameters:

  • row (dict) – Source record dictionary.

  • keys (str) – Candidate field names, tried in order.

Returns:

First matching value, or None if none found.

Return type:

object | None

load_data.read_json_or_jsonl(file_path)

Read a JSON array/object file or JSONL file into record dicts.

Parameters:

file_path (str | pathlib.Path) – Path to the input file.

Returns:

Normalized list of applicant records.

Return type:

list[dict]
